page: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 2.6 GHz AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU with no hyperthreading. The CPU includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1505.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1380.0. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks are limited, resulting in a stress-ng multi-core score of 1505.29 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1384.0. Memory performance shows a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 96.98 GB/sec, while uncached large block write bandwidth is lower at 10.44 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average rate of 23.64 GB/sec, whereas bzip3 decompression is weaker at 8.39 GB/sec.

The c7g.medium offers a cost-effective entry point into the ARM64 Graviton ecosystem, delivering dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of multi-threaded virtualization. The primary tradeoff is the strict resource constraint of a single vCPU and 2.0 GB of RAM, which limits its utility for parallel processing, large-scale database operations, or memory-heavy applications. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, small-scale Redis caching, and simple utility scripts or microservices that benefit from dedicated ARM64 compute resources.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is an entry-level, compute-optimized instance utilizing a dedicated 2.6 GHz AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor. Built on the ARM64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1380.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 23.64 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory write speeds are constrained by the single-vCPU configuration. This instance offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale microservices requiring dedicated ARM64 compute resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a dedicated 2.6 GHz AWS Neoverse-V1 processor, 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with a dedicated 2.6 GHz Neoverse-V1 processor, 1 vCPU, and 2.0 GB of RAM.
tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 compute instance designed for lightweight, cost-efficient single-threaded workloads, microservices, and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Dedicated 2.6 GHz AWS ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor (ARM64)
1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and single-threaded tasks
